title: Cambridge pediatric doctor running fifth Boston Marathon to support Mass General patients
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Cambridge resident Jennifer Leah Goetz is running her fifth Boston Marathon to support Massachusetts General Hospital patients.
Goetz, 39, trained in pediatrics and child psychiatry at MGH, working with children battling cancer.
She started running to overcome depression and now raises funds for MGH through the marathon.

title: Brothers unite for Boston Marathon run honoring family, cancer losses
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Collin and Conor Nally are running the 130th Boston Marathon in honor of family members lost to cancer.
Collin began running after his father and fiancé's mother died from cancer, using it as a way to cope.
The brothers aim to use their ability to run to honor those impacted by cancer in their Boston Marathon effort.

title: Mass. has one of the best minor league ballparks in America, according to USA Today readers
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Polar Park in Worcester, home to the Worcester Red Sox, ranked No. 6 in USA Today's 2026 10Best Readers' Choice Awards.
USA Today praised Polar Park for its atmosphere, architecture, and role in revitalizing Worcester since opening in 2021.
Worcester Red Sox mascot Smiley Ball ranked No. 3 among the best minor league mascots in the U.S. by USA Today readers.

title: 5 best new restaurant openings and dining updates in Boston (March 26, 2026)
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: 7th Street Burger from NYC opens on Newbury St. in Boston; South Boston location to open April 26.
Far Out Ice Cream expands in Greater Boston, opening its 4th location in Brighton on April 19.
Gemma Kitchen & Bar, featuring American-Italian cuisine, set to open in Norwood's old firehouse in late April.

title: State Police Sgt. Scott Quigley indicted in deadly Woburn crash
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: State Police Sgt. Scott Quigley indicted for motor vehicle homicide in 2023 crash that killed Angelo Schettino in Woburn.
Quigley allegedly drove drunk and crashed into a van, injuring the driver and fatally injuring Schettino, a disabled passenger.
Prosecution handled by Suffolk DA due to Quigley's work in Middlesex; arraignment set for Middlesex Superior Court.

title: Framingham’s iconic ‘castle’ hotel has entered a new era. Take a look inside.
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Framingham Hotel Collection has opened after renovations, featuring three hotels: Renaissance, Fairfield Inn & Suites, and TownePlace Suites.
The Renaissance Framingham Hotel offers 25,000 square feet of event space, blending European architecture with modern amenities.
Dining options include Malone’s Irish Pub & Social, Provisions Hearth & Kitchen, and Tara Cafe, catering to diverse culinary preferences.

title: With travel concerns rising, are you rethinking flights?
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Concerns over TSA staffing at Logan Airport rise amid Department of Homeland Security shutdown effects.
Union leaders warn staffing strain could cause travel delays as summer approaches.
Recent LaGuardia crash raises safety concerns, tied to staffing and technology failures.

title: This Boston hotel is giving music lovers a reason to travel
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Omni Boston Hotel at the Seaport offers an All Access Pass package with concert tickets even for sold-out events.
TSA has implemented a new biometric system at Logan Airport, allowing PreCheck flyers to verify identity using facial recognition.
Coastal Maine Botanical Garden in Boothbay was ranked among the best gardens in the U.S. by USA Today readers.

title: Tom Brady reveals that he has ‘explored a lot of different things’ about making an NFL comeback
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Tom Brady, 48, considered an NFL return but must divest his Raiders stake to play.
Brady inquired about NFL rules for a playing return as a minority owner, facing league resistance.
Despite retirement, Brady enjoys other pursuits, including Raiders ownership and broadcasting with Fox.

title: Police release bodycam footage of Plymouth butcher knife standoff
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Plymouth police released bodycam footage of a standoff where a man held his estranged wife at knifepoint.
The incident occurred on Clark Road and involved four children, who officers evacuated safely during negotiations.
The suspect, facing over a dozen charges, surrendered after 45 minutes of negotiation with the police.
